Permanently deletes the album from the device.
On Android, it deletes the album and all its assets.
On iOS, it deletes the album but keeps the assets in the main library.

summary.text:
Removes assets from the album without deleting them from the library.
This is supported only on iOS.

On Android, an asset can belong to only one album. To remove it from an album,
delete it or add it to another album.

summary.text:
Represents a query to fetch data from the media library.

A

implements a builder pattern, allowing you to chain multiple filtering and sorting methods
to construct complex queries.
Filters assets to only those contained in the specified album.

content.text: The updated query object for chaining.

summary.text: The album to filter assets by.
Filters assets where the specified field is equal to the given value.

content.text: The updated query object for chaining.

summary.text: The value that the field should equal. Each field has a specific unique type.
Executes the query and retrieves the matching assets.

content.text: A promise that resolves to an array of [
](#asset) objects that match the query criteria.

```ts
const assets = await new Query()
 .eq(AssetField.MEDIA_TYPE, MediaType.IMAGE)
 .lte(AssetField.HEIGHT, 1080)
 .orderBy(AssetField.CREATION_TIME)
 .limit(20)
 .exe();
```

summary.text:
Executes the query and retrieves lightweight metadata for the matching assets.

Returns fields that can be read cheaply from the media store, without resolving file paths or
decoding files.

content.text: A promise that resolves to an array of [
](#assetmetadata) objects that match the query criteria.

```ts
const assets = await new Query()
 .eq(AssetField.MEDIA_TYPE, MediaType.IMAGE)
 .lte(AssetField.HEIGHT, 1080)
 .orderBy(AssetField.CREATION_TIME)
 .limit(20)
 .exeForMetadata();
```

After calling this function, the listener will no longer receive any events from the emitter.
